summary: Osmium blue cannot be a stain used in tattoos because it lacks stability required for long-term color retention; instead, it serves specialized scientific purposes like preserving tissue structure at microscopic levels which do not apply to decorative body art needs involving pigment application via needles under repeated sun exposure conditions conducive towards fading/alteration typical across most commercial dyes employed today ensuring vibrant appearance lasting decades post-application despite occasional maintenance treatments needed due inherent nature mutable organic compounds constituting majority such formulations designed specifically withstand environmental factors degrading aesthetics otherwise compromising overall customer experience expectations associated
